title: Canary — Browser QA
description: Describe a user flow in plain language; the agent drives a real browser through it and hands back a self-contained QA report (per-step screenshots, console, network HAR, Playwright trace) AND a reusable replay.py script that re-runs the flow in CI with zero agent cost.

cta.label: Fork and run
cta.note: Runs on Jetty's managed sandbox. No setup. Free for your first 10 runs.

example_outputs.title: TodoMVC — add & complete a todo
example_outputs.summary: Add two todos and complete one. 7/7 steps pass, with real assertions on the counter (2 → 1) and the completed state. Self-contained report + replay.py.

input.flow: Add two todos, complete one, assert the counter

outputs.description: Self-contained QA report (per-step screenshots inlined, verdict).
Reusable Playwright script that reproduces the flow in CI.

example_outputs.title: SauceDemo — login & add to cart
example_outputs.summary: Log in to a demo store, add an item, assert the cart badge. 7/7 steps pass — login lands on the inventory page and the cart badge shows 1.

input.flow: Log in, add the backpack to the cart, assert the cart badge

example_outputs.title: the-internet — login & logout
example_outputs.summary: Log in on a QA playground, assert the success flash banner, then log out and assert the form returns. 7/7 steps pass.

input.flow: Log in, assert the success banner, log out, assert the form returns

inputs.label: Flow
inputs.description: The user journey to QA in plain language, with the checks that must hold (visible text / URL / state / no console error).

attribution.author: Canary contributors (usecanary) — github.com/wizenheimer/canary
attribution.note: Canary ships a CLI + a QuickJS-WASM Playwright sandbox + a daemon; this runbook re-implements its core idea (describe a flow → agent drives → report + reusable script) with plain Playwright so it runs in the Jetty sandbox.

caveats: This runbook re-implements the canary concept with plain Playwright; it is not the canary CLI/QuickJS-WASM runtime. The deliverables (report + screenshots + HAR + trace + a reusable replay.py) match canary's value, but the on-disk format differs from canary's own report.html / session layout.

The runbook produces a full network.har and a Playwright trace.zip per run, but those heavy binaries (6–10 MB/run) are omitted from the shipped examples to keep the repo lean — they're reproducible by re-running. report.html (screenshots inlined), replay.py, and the per-step screenshots ARE shipped.
Best on deterministic, public, server-rendered test apps with stable selectors. Auth-walled or heavily dynamic SPAs may need credentials and more retries.
Drives a live site, so a flow can change if the target changes.

handoff.name: wizenheimer-canary
handoff.eval_runs: 3/3 production runs green (c5172934, 8b80f8a0, affed313), each 7/7 steps PASS with report.html + a reusable replay.py
